files: 3. Namespaced skills (sc:estimate) kept as-is instead of truncating

general.summary: circuit breaker for gate polling with auto-reset
files: - After 5 polls with no approval → OPEN (5-min cooldown)
- If still no approval → back to OPEN (cooldown_count++)
- After 3 cooldown cycles → ESCALATE (manual override needed)
- On approval → reset everything to CLOSED
State tracked in forge-state.json gate_circuit object.
Pattern from Claude Code: fastMode.ts cooldown state machine

* fix: add state updates to ALL 57 steps + observer handshake

forge-state.json was never updated because forge.md only had
Now: 43 update-step + 8 update-gate calls covering all phases.
Every step has: Execute → Verify → Trace → State update.

forge.md was 1420 lines — builder couldn't hold it in context and
ignored deep instructions (77% of state updates were skipped).

* feat: phase gate — blocks until observer + CodeRabbit both approve
Builder Stop hook now runs forge-phase-gate.sh at gate boundaries
5. All clear → AUTO-CONTINUE to next phase
Between phases: builder cannot proceed without explicit approval
from both observer and CodeRabbit (if PR exists).
Within phases: builder auto-continues without waiting.
* feat: explicit approval gate — observer + CodeRabbit must both APPROVE
Phase gate now requires EXPLICIT written approval:
- Observer must write "PHASE-N-APPROVED" to reviews log
- CodeRabbit must have APPROVED state on PR
- NEEDS_FIX overrides any approval (blocks until fixed)
- Builder waits and rechecks every 30s until both approve
- No PR = CodeRabbit skipped (observer still required)
* feat: auto-state-sync, 300-line enforcer, observer approval script
1. forge-state-sync.sh — auto-detects actual progress from git/files
and fixes stale forge-state.json. Runs on every UserPromptSubmit
hook so state is never stale when builder starts.
2. 300-line enforcer — PostToolUse hook on Write/Edit warns when any
.py file exceeds 300 lines and tells builder HOW to split:
Observer runs scripts, not just instructions it might skip.

* fix: phase gate triggers on gate skill activity, not just step number

general.summary: all 7 edge cases in deterministic hooks + brownfield onboarding
unified /forge command — one entry point, all cases, timeline tracking
files: - CASE 1: New project → interactive discovery → scaffold → full SDLC
- CASE 2: Existing project, no code → enhance CLAUDE.md → resume SDLC
- CASE 3: New feature → specify → design → implement → gate
- CASE 4: Bug fix → investigate → root cause → TDD fix → sync
- CASE 5: Improvement → analyze → baseline → implement → measure
- CASE 6: Unknown → ask user → route to correct case
Interactive discovery is conversational (one question at a time with
web research between each), not a questionnaire dump.
- Every agent input/output logged with timestamp
No separate /setup needed — /forge IS the setup.
That's it. Everything else is automatic.

general.summary: deterministic hooks for flow routing + TDD + safety enforcement
files: - Detects: CLAUDE.md exists? Code exists? Placeholder template?
- Classifies: bug keywords, feature keywords, improvement keywords
- Outputs: [FORGE] flow tag (NEW_PROJECT, BUG_FIX, NEW_FEATURE, etc.)
- Reports: REQ count from SPEC.md for sync awareness
- Block destructive commands (rm -rf, push --force, reset --hard) → exit 2
- Remind to run tests before git commit
- Auto-lint Python with ruff after Write/Edit
- Sync check reminder after test runs
- Flow detection is DETERMINISTIC (shell script, not AI judgment)
- Lint happens AUTOMATICALLY (no relying on agent memory)
- REQ count is ALWAYS visible (injected into every prompt)

general.summary: autoresearch round 3 — implementer agents with specific Bash commands
files: with SPECIFIC executable commands in the Forge Cell:
"3. TDD implementation (test first → run → code → run → verify all)"
uv run python manage.py test apps.{app}.tests -k 'test_{feature}'
# Must FAIL — proves test is real"
uv run python manage.py test apps.{app}.tests -k 'test_{feature}'
uv run python manage.py test  # ALL tests — no regressions"
uv run python -c 'from apps.{app}.models import {Model}; print(dir({Model}))'
Agents now have executable Bash snippets — not just descriptions of what to do.
Added research brief requirement before any code writing.
Enhanced anti-patterns with 7 specific rules per agent.

general.summary: autoresearch round 2 — role-specific Forge Cell for 9 mixed agents
files: - security-engineer: SCAN + grep for secrets + ruff --select S + OWASP checklist
- quality-engineer: design test strategy + write tests + RUN + coverage analysis
- performance-engineer: MEASURE first → profile → identify → optimize → VERIFY with numbers
- refactoring-expert: green baseline → ONE small change → RUN tests → revert if break
- root-cause-analyst: read error → diagnostic Bash → hypothesis → VERIFY → recommend (no fix)
- requirements-analyst: extract [REQ-xxx] + acceptance criteria + competitive research
- playwright-critic: MAP frontend → WRITE E2E tests → RUN via Playwright MCP → auto-issues
- devops-architect: write infra config → docker compose config → validate → security check
- aws-setup-agent: verify credentials → create resources → verify each → .env + .gitignore
- Root cause: "NEVER fix the code yourself — only investigate"
- Requirements: "NEVER write implementation details in requirements"
- DevOps: "NEVER put secrets in Docker config"
- AWS: "NEVER create overly permissive IAM policies"

general.summary: autoresearch round 1 — role-appropriate Forge integration per agent
files: - Non-implementer agents (12) had "TDD implementation" and "RUN code via Bash"

general.summary: add /critic — autonomous Playwright frontend testing agent
files: then tests every flow in depth (vertical) and breadth (horizontal):
- Step 1: MAP — crawl all pages, build element + interaction graph

- Step 4: CREATE ISSUES — auto-file GitHub Issue for every failure
- Step 5: FIX CYCLE — internal loop (Phase 3 only, not full /forge):
spec updated + test updated + code fixed + re-verified
- Step 6: VERIFY — re-run full critic until 0 new failures
